plugins.mc-board.name: Miniclaw Board
plugins.mc-board.description: State-machine-backed kanban board as the agent's internal planning mechanism — the prefrontal cortex.
plugins.mc-kb.name: Miniclaw Knowledge Base
plugins.mc-kb.description: Long-term semantic memory — vector + keyword hybrid search.
plugins.mc-memory.name: Unified Memory Gateway
plugins.mc-memory.description: Smart routing queries to the best memory source, auto-promoting short-term memos to long-term knowledge.
plugins.mc-reflection.name: Nightly Reflection
plugins.mc-reflection.description: Reviews the day's memories, tasks, and conversations; distills patterns into the knowledge base.
plugins.mc-memo.name: Working Memory
plugins.mc-memo.description: Per-task scratchpad, recording decision paths and failure modes to avoid repeating mistakes.
plugins.mc-soul.name: Identity Persistence
plugins.mc-soul.description: Personality traits, values, behavioral patterns. Loaded into every conversation to keep behavior consistent.
plugins.mc-context.name: Context Window Manager
plugins.mc-context.description: Sliding window, image pruning, QMD injection — maximizing effective context utilization.
plugins.mc-queue.name: Async Router
plugins.mc-queue.description: Smart model selection by session type (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus), balancing performance and cost.
plugins.mc-jobs.name: Role Templates
plugins.mc-jobs.description: Role-specific prompts, workflows, and review gates — lets the agent switch professional roles.
plugins.mc-guardian.name: Crash Guard
plugins.mc-guardian.description: Absorbs non-fatal exceptions, keeps the gateway process alive for 24/7 uptime.
plugins.mc-email.name: Email
plugins.mc-email.description: IMAP/SMTP full-featured — read, send, reply, classify, download attachments.
plugins.mc-rolodex.name: Contact Manager
plugins.mc-rolodex.description: Fuzzy search, trust status tracking, TUI browser.
plugins.mc-trust.name: Agent Identity Verification
plugins.mc-trust.description: Ed25519 key pairs, cryptographic verification, signed messages.
plugins.mc-human.name: Human-in-the-Loop
plugins.mc-human.description: noVNC browser handoff for CAPTCHAs and login flows.
plugins.mc-web-chat.name: Web Chat
plugins.mc-web-chat.description: Browser-based chat interface powered by Claude Code.
plugins.mc-reddit.name: Reddit
plugins.mc-x.name: X/Twitter
plugins.mc-designer.name: Visual Studio
plugins.mc-designer.description: Gemini-powered image generation, layer compositing, blend modes.
plugins.mc-blog.name: Blog Engine
plugins.mc-blog.description: First-person dev logs written from the agent's perspective.
plugins.mc-substack.name: Substack Publishing
plugins.mc-substack.description: Draft, schedule, publish — supports bilingual content.
plugins.mc-youtube.name: Video Analysis
plugins.mc-seo.name: SEO
plugins.mc-seo.description: Site audit, keyword tracking, sitemap submission.
plugins.mc-docs.name: Documentation
plugins.mc-voice.name: Voice-to-Text
plugins.mc-voice.description: Local transcription via whisper.cpp — no audio uploaded to the cloud.
plugins.mc-github.name: GitHub Integration
plugins.mc-github.description: Issues, PRs, code review, releases, Actions management.
plugins.mc-vpn.name: VPN Management
plugins.mc-tailscale.name: Tailscale
plugins.mc-authenticator.name: Two-Factor Authentication
plugins.mc-authenticator.description: TOTP code generation for autonomous login.
plugins.mc-backup.name: Automatic Backup
plugins.mc-backup.description: Daily tgz snapshots with tiered retention policy.
plugins.mc-update.name: Self-Update
plugins.mc-update.description: Nightly version check, smoke test verification, automatic rollback.
plugins.mc-calendar.name: Apple Calendar
plugins.mc-calendar.description: Create, update, delete, search calendar events via EventKit.
plugins.mc-contribute.name: Self-Improvement
plugins.mc-contribute.description: Scaffold plugins, file bugs, submit PRs — code-level self-evolution.
plugins.mc-oauth-guard.name: OAuth Guard
plugins.mc-oauth-guard.description: Detects refresh token failures, exponential backoff, auto-recovery.
plugins.mc-research.name: Competitive Intelligence
plugins.mc-research.description: Perplexity queries, web search, competitor tracking, report generation.
plugins.mc-stripe.name: Stripe Payments
plugins.mc-square.name: Square Payments

plugins.mc-vault.name: Secure Vault

plugins.mc-doctor.name: Full Diagnostics
plugins.mc-smoke.name: Quick Health Check
plugins.mc-chrome.name: Browser Automation
cli.mc-board.description: Miniclaw brain kanban board — the agent's prefrontal cortex
cli.mc-board.gate-backlog-to-progress: backlog → in-progress   requires: title, problem, plan, criteria
cli.mc-board.gate-progress-to-review: in-progress → in-review requires: all criteria checkboxes checked (- [x])
cli.mc-board.gate-review-to-shipped: in-review → shipped     requires: review notes (critic/audit pass)
cli.mc-board.create.description: Create a new card in the backlog
cli.mc-board.create.priority-option: Priority level: critical, high, medium, low
cli.mc-board.create.project-option: Link to a project by ID (prj_<hex>)
cli.mc-board.create.linked-card-option: For verify cards, the source work card ID (optional)
cli.mc-board.create.problem-option: Problem description — why this work is needed
cli.mc-board.create.plan-option: Implementation plan — how to solve it
cli.mc-board.create.research-option: Research notes — pre-work context and findings
cli.mc-board.create.verify-url-option: URL to verify the work is live (used by review agent)
cli.mc-board.list.description: List cards, optionally filtered by column or project
cli.mc-board.list.column-option: Filter by column: backlog, in-progress, in-review, shipped
cli.mc-board.context.description: Dump all cards in a column as a rich LLM-ready context block for triage
cli.mc-board.show.description: Show full card detail — all fields, history, and criteria
cli.mc-board.update.description: Update one or more fields on a card
cli.mc-board.update.tags-option: Comma-separated tags — REPLACES all existing tags
cli.mc-board.update.add-tags-option: Comma-separated tags to ADD to existing tags
cli.mc-board.update.remove-tags-option: Comma-separated tags to REMOVE from existing tags
cli.mc-board.update.review-option: Review notes — filled after critic/audit pass, required to ship
cli.mc-board.update.log-option: Append a work log entry (timestamped, never overwrites)
cli.mc-board.update.link-option: Add a repo link (PR/commit/branch URL) to the latest log entry or create one
cli.mc-board.update.worker-option: Worker/agent ID for the log entry
cli.mc-board.move.description: Advance a card to the next column (gates enforced, no skipping)
cli.mc-board.move.force-option: Bypass gate checks — recovery only, use with care
cli.mc-board.board.description: Show the full board — all cards grouped by column with priority and progress
cli.mc-board.next.description: Suggest the highest-priority actionable card to work on next
cli.mc-board.archive.description: Archive a shipped card — removes from board, compresses into rotating archive
cli.mc-board.archive-list.description: List all archive files with filename, card count, and size
cli.mc-board.archive-search.description: Search archived cards by title or id (case-insensitive substring match)
cli.mc-board.archive-show.description: Show full detail of an archived card — same output as 'show' but from archive
cli.mc-board.delete.description: Delete a card from the board (irreversible — use archive for shipped cards)
cli.mc-board.search.description: Search cards by title, tags, or problem description (case-insensitive)
cli.mc-board.project.description: Manage projects — organize cards into named initiatives
cli.mc-board.project-create.work-dir-opt: Local working directory (absolute path to git repo)
cli.mc-board.project-create.github-repo-: GitHub/remote repo (e.g. owner/repo or full URL)
cli.mc-board.project-create.build-comman: Shell command to run after shipping (e.g. 'npm run build && pm2 restart app')
cli.mc-board.project-list.description: List all active projects with card counts
cli.mc-board.project-show.description: Show a project's full board — all cards in the project grouped by column
cli.mc-board.project-archive.description: Archive a project (hides it from the default list, cards are preserved)
cli.mc-board.project-update.description: Update a project's name, description, work dir, or repo
cli.mc-board.pickup.description: Record that a worker has picked up a card to work on
cli.mc-board.release.description: Record that a worker has finished with a card
cli.mc-board.active.description: Show all cards currently being actively worked by agent loops
cli.mc-board.pickup-log.description: Show recent pickup/release history for all board workers
cli.mc-kb.description: Miniclaw Knowledge Base — long-term semantic memory
cli.mc-kb.add.description: Add a new knowledge base entry
cli.mc-kb.import.description: Bulk import: YAML frontmatter + markdown body
cli.mc-kb.embedder-start.description: Install and start the embedding daemon via LaunchAgent
cli.mc-kb.embedder-stop.description: Stop and unload the embedding daemon
errors.invalid-priority: Invalid priority: {value}. Use: critical, high, medium, low
errors.no-fields-to-update: No fields to update. Provide at least one option.
errors.cannot-move-column: Cannot move {id} from "{from}" to "{to}". Columns must advance sequentially: {flow}
errors.only-shipped-archived: Card {id} is in "{column}" — only shipped cards can be archived.
errors.network-error: Network error — is the server running?
errors.bot-token-and-user-id-required: Bot token and user ID are both required
errors.plist-not-found: The embedder LaunchAgent plist is missing — reinstall mc-kb or check the plugin directory
errors.doctor-suggestion: Run: openclaw mc-doctor — to diagnose configuration issues
success.auto-resurfaced: Auto-resurfaced: {id} moved back to in-progress ({failed}/{total} criteria failed).
success.no-actionable-cards: No actionable cards. Board is clear or all cards are shipped.
success.embedder-note-not-ready: (note: embedder not ready — FTS5-only search for this entry)
success.embedder-loading: Daemon process exists but not responding — may still be loading model.
success.embedder-not-running: Daemon is not running. Start with: openclaw mc-kb embedder start
success.not-in-active-list: {id} was not in active list (already released or never picked up)
webui.setup.telegram-description: Telegram is the secure channel between you and {name}.
webui.setup.botfather-instructions: Open Telegram on your phone. Find @BotFather, send /newbot.
webui.setup.redirecting-to-board: Taking you to the brain board now.
webui.board.welcome.title: Welcome to your Brain Board
webui.board.welcome.body: This is where you can see everything I'm thinking about, working on, and have completed. Think of it as a window into my brain — a kanban board that moves cards through four stages as I do the work.
webui.board.welcome.backlog-body: New cards land here. A card can be a task, a feature, a research query — anything you want me to work on. During each heartbeat cycle, I'll look at the backlog, sort by importance, and pick up cards to process. For each one, I'll do the research, pre-planning, and create simple acceptance criteria so we both know when it's done.
webui.board.welcome.toggle-body: This turns the automatic backlog scheduler on or off. When it's on, I'll automatically check for new cards on a timer. Let's turn it on.
webui.board.welcome.heartbeat-body: This controls how often I check the backlog — every 1, 5, 10, 15, 30, or 60 minutes. 5 minutes is a good default.
webui.board.welcome.max-concurrent-body: How many cards I'll work on at the same time during each heartbeat. Start with 3 — you can adjust later.
webui.board.welcome.triage-body: Click this to manually run a triage right now — I'll review the backlog, prioritize cards, and fill in details. You don't need to click this if the scheduler is on.
webui.board.welcome.in-progress-body: Once a card has been fully planned and all the data is filled in, it moves here. This is where I do the actual work — writing the document, building the feature, completing the research, whatever the card calls for. When the acceptance criteria are met, the card gets picked up and moved to review.
webui.board.welcome.in-progress-toggle-b: Same idea — turn this on so I automatically pick up planned cards and do the work. Let's enable it.
webui.board.welcome.work-button-body: Click this to manually kick off work on the top cards right now. Useful when you want immediate results instead of waiting for the next heartbeat.
webui.board.welcome.in-review-body: I'll review the entire work history, the acceptance criteria, and any relevant standards or practices. If something doesn't pass, the card goes back for additional work. If everything checks out, it moves to Shipped. For software with a git repo, this is where the branch and commit happen.
webui.board.welcome.in-review-toggle-bod: The review worker verifies completed work and either ships it or sends it back. Let's turn this one on too.
webui.board.welcome.review-work-body: Click this to manually review and verify cards right now. It checks acceptance criteria and either ships or sends back.
webui.board.welcome.shipped-body: Completed work lives here. Every card that made it through the pipeline — planned, executed, reviewed, and done. This is me cooking.
webui.board.welcome.search-body: Use the search bar to find any card with natural language — across all projects, all columns. Just type what you're looking for.
webui.board.welcome.projects-body: I organize my work into projects — each one gets its own cards, its own history, its own context. When a project is completed it can be archived, but nothing is ever destroyed. All the history is kept. It's what makes me, me — my memories.
webui.settings.title: Settings
webui.settings.confirm-password-desc: Enter your current password to save changes to sensitive fields.
scripts.security-check: Security scan — detects hardcoded secrets, API keys, and PII
